name: Old-Time Boiled Custard
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix milk, heavy cream, and salt over a double boiler.
2. Cook over simmering water, stirring to dissolve sugar until the mixture is nearly scalding (bubbles will form at the edge of the liquid).
3. Remove from heat. In a small bowl, beat the eggs with a wire whisk until foamy.
4. Gradually whisk about 1/3 cup of the milk mixture into the eggs.
5. Gradually whisk the warmed eggs into the hot milk, beating constantly.
6. Continue to cook over simmering water, stirring almost constantly, until the mixture coats the back of a spoon (10-15 minutes).
7. Remove from heat and stir in 1/2 teaspoon vanilla.
8. Cover the surface of the custard with plastic wrap, then chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
9. Serve dolloped on pieces of Mama Crow's Raisin-Nut Jam Cake (see separate recipe). Makes about 2 cups.
- Per tablespoon: 29 calories, 1 gram protein, 2 grams fat, 3 grams carbohydrate.
